scenario.title: Fleet-shaped loop proposals with provenance and platform adaptation
steps: User creates a loop whose intent matches a cached shape from another instance
The proposal card shows a provenance badge: 'Based on your X loop on Y, adapted for Z'
Platform-specific substitutions (gh↔az) are applied to the proposed command

assertions.description: matchShapeToFleetIntent finds exact command matches across instances (score 0.9)
matchShapeToFleetIntent finds base-command matches (score 0.5)
matchShapeToFleetIntent skips own-environment shapes
matchShapeToFleetIntent returns null when no shapes match
detectCommandPlatform detects 'gh' commands as GitHub
detectCommandPlatform detects 'az' commands as ADO
detectCommandPlatform detects github.com URLs as GitHub
detectCommandPlatform detects dev.azure.com URLs as ADO
detectCommandPlatform returns 'unknown' for neutral commands
adaptShapeForPlatform adapts gh → az when target is ADO
adaptShapeForPlatform adapts az → gh when target is GitHub
adaptShapeForPlatform adapts chain step commands
adaptShapeForPlatform adapts GitHub URLs to Azure DevOps URLs
adaptShapeForPlatform leaves neutral commands unchanged
adaptShapeForPlatform returns unchanged shape for same platform
adaptShapeForPlatform records all substitution metadata correctly
buildProvenance produces 'Based on your X loop on Y, adapted for Z' format
buildProvenance omits platform label when no substitutions were made
buildProvenance uses GitHub/Azure DevOps platform labels correctly
LoopProposalRow carries provenance and adaptedFrom fields (type-level)
LoopProposalCard renders provenance badge when row.provenance is set
Provenance badge CSS styles are defined in theme.css
i18n keys for provenance are added to en.json
Mock LoopShape data includes a second environment with GitHub commands
FleetShapedProposalCard wrapper computes shape match + adaptation async
TypeScript compilation passes with no errors across all changed files
All 26 fleet-shape-adapt unit tests pass
Existing fleet-similarity tests (21 passing) are not affected

prMarkdown:
## Visual Evidence

### Fleet-shaped loop proposals with provenance and platform adaptation

> Screenshot could not be captured in this headless environment. See unit tests (26 new + 21 existing all passing) for behavioral verification.

**Scenario:** When a user creates a loop whose intent matches a cached `LoopShape` from another instance, the proposal card pre-shapes the loop using the matched shape's chain structure and adapts platform-specific parts (e.g. `gh` → `az`) using the project's detected platform. A provenance badge appears on the card: "Based on your implement-issue loop on prod-vm, adapted for Azure DevOps".

**Key changes:**
- New `fleet-shape-adapt.ts` module with pure shape matching (`matchShapeToFleetIntent`) and platform adaptation (`adaptShapeForPlatform`) functions
- `gh↔az` CLI substitution rules, URL host swaps (`github.com↔dev.azure.com`), and arg style adaptations (`--repo↔--organization`)
- `LoopProposalRow` extended with `provenance` and `adaptedFrom` fields
- `FleetShapedProposalCard` wrapper component that async-computes shape match + platform adaptation for pending proposals
- `LoopProposalCard` renders a provenance badge under the header when `row.provenance` is set
- `buildProvenance()` generates human-readable provenance strings stating source loop, instance, and adapted platform
- 26 Vitest unit tests for all new functions (all passing)
- Mock data includes a second environment with GitHub-flavoured commands for cross-platform testing

**Acceptance criteria met:**
- ✅ When creation intent matches a cached shape, the proposal reuses the chain structure and adapts platform-specific parts using the project's detected platform
- ✅ The proposal states provenance ('based on your implement-issue loop on prod-vm, adapted for Azure DevOps')
- ✅ Standard approve-before-create applies (proposals still go through the normal approve/reject flow)
